body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no" ?>
<SnowReport>
	<_LastUpdated>2026-07-24T07:14:25</_LastUpdated>
	<TwentyFourHourSnowfall>3</TwentyFourHourSnowfall>
	<CurrentSnowdepth>42</CurrentSnowdepth>
	<AvSnowdepthinSnowmakingAreas>107</AvSnowdepthinSnowmakingAreas>
	<AvSnowdepthLastMeasuredOn>23/07/2026</AvSnowdepthLastMeasuredOn>
	<DateofLastSnowfall>24/07/2026</DateofLastSnowfall>
	<SevenDaySnowfall>5</SevenDaySnowfall>
	<SeasonToDateSnowfall>101</SeasonToDateSnowfall>
	<Snowmaking>In Progress</Snowmaking>
	<WeatherConditionsasat6AM></WeatherConditionsasat6AM>
	<Temperature>-4.6</Temperature>
	<OvernightMin>-5.4</OvernightMin>
	<ForecastMax>-1</ForecastMax>
	<Wind>Moderate/Strong 41-60 km/h</Wind>
	<WindDirection>SSW</WindDirection>
	<Precipitation>Snow Flurries</Precipitation>
	<ExpectedVisibility>Fair</ExpectedVisibility>
	<ExpectedCloudCover>Cloudy</ExpectedCloudCover>
	<Forecast>Cloudy. Medium chance of snow showers. Winds southwesterly 25 to 40 km/h tending westerly 20 to 30 km/h in the middle of the day then increasing to 30 to 40 km/h in the evening.</Forecast>
	<ResortReportHeadline>Wintery Blast!</ResortReportHeadline>
	<ResortReport><p>It really feels like winter this morning!&nbsp; We've picked up a few extra centimeters in the past 24 hours and there is still the odd flurry as I write.&nbsp; The temperature is a chilly minus 4.6, and the wind is a strong southerly.&nbsp; The snow guns have been running continuously for over 24 hours, which should have made a big difference to the cover in many areas.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><p>The groomers will still be the best bet for fun turns this morning, but the forecast is showing snow showers for the next few days and hopefully a significant dump early in the week.&nbsp; Powder turns may not be too far away?&nbsp; Stay tuned!&nbsp;&nbsp;</p><p>Heavenly Valley area is open but the cover is thin, the snow is hard and the runs are only suitable for advanced and expert skiers and riders. The extreme area is closed due to a significant ice hazard, and please do not attempt to retrieve dropped equipment from closed areas of the resort.</p><p>Road Runner is scheduled for First Tracks from 7.30am this morning.&nbsp;</p><p>Rug up for the significant windchill and get out there.</p><p>Cheers Bill</p></ResortReport>
	<HideReportEDMWeb>False</HideReportEDMWeb>
	<LiftsOpen></LiftsOpen>
	<LiftsStandby></LiftsStandby>
	<LiftsClosed></LiftsClosed>
	<RunsOpen></RunsOpen>
	<RunsGroomed></RunsGroomed>
	<RunsSnowmaking>5</RunsSnowmaking>
	<TerrainParks></TerrainParks>
	<Backcountry>24 July 2026

There is a significant sliding hazard in the backcountry again today, plus the cover is thin, becoming more marginal the lower you go. All aspects will likely stay very icy and dangerous for the day. There has not been enough fresh snow to reduce the sliding hazard. Take Care. </Backcountry>
	<Avalanche></Avalanche>
	<HeliLink>Closed</HeliLink>
</SnowReport>
